responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to provide a "security boxe" (security box) for sharing credentials. Based on the conversation context, they want a secure way to provide credentials for the IVX deployment pipeline.

Let me look at the conversation history - they've been working on deploying IVX and had issues with credentials being empty. The user wants a secure interface to enter their GitHub token, Render API key, etc.

I should use the `requestEnvs` tool to create a secure credential input interface. Let me identify what credentials are needed based on the previous conversation and the private environment variables listed.

Looking at the private environment variables:
- GITHUB_TOKEN
- RENDER_API_KEY
- RENDER_SERVICE_ID
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- SUPABASE_URL

Several of these are showing as empty. Let me present a credential input form.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) with `repo` scope — used to push code and trigger Render deploys

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API Key from Dashboard → Account → API Keys

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render Web Service ID — find in your Render dashboard URL: dashboard.render.com/web/srv-XXXXX

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service_role key from Project Settings → API

input.reason: These credentials are needed to push to GitHub, trigger Render deploys, and verify the Supabase database. Enter them once and IVX will auto-deploy continuously.

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requestedEnvs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) with `repo` scope — used to push code and trigger Render deploys

requestedEnvs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Render API Key from Dashboard → Account → API Keys

requestedEnvs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
requestedEnvs.description: Render Web Service ID — find in your Render dashboard URL: dashboard.render.com/web/srv-XXXXX

requestedEnvs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Supabase service_role key from Project Settings → API

output.reason: These credentials are needed to push to GitHub, trigger Render deploys, and verify the Supabase database. Enter them once and IVX will auto-deploy continuously.

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"envs": [{"name": "GITHUB_TOKEN", "description": "GitHub Personal Access Token (classic) with `repo` scope — used to push code and trigger Render deploys", "placeholder": "ghp_...", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "RENDER_API_KEY", "description": "Render API Key from Dashboard → Account → API Keys", "placeholder": "rnd_...", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "RENDER_SERVICE_ID", "description": "Render Web Service ID — find in your Render dashboard URL: dashboard.render.com/web/srv-XXXXX", "placeholder": "srv-...", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "SUPABASE_URL", "description": "Supabase project URL (e.g. https://xxxxx.supabase.co)", "placeholder": "https://xxxxx.supabase.co", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY", "description": "Supabase service_role key from Project Settings → API", "placeholder": "eyJ...", "isPublic": false}], "reason": "These credentials are needed to push to GitHub, trigger Render deploys, and verify the Supabase database. Enter them once and IVX will auto-deploy continuously."}
